source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals

confidence_rationale: Direct mechanistic anchor for the imagination-side rollout substrate. Pfeiffer & Foster show rat hippocampus generates brief place-cell sequences before goal-directed navigation that encode future trajectories from current location to remembered goal -- the rodent analog of REE's hippocampal rollout substrate (ARC-007 / SD-002). The trajectories elaborate dynamically as the agent approaches prediction-relevant cues, which is precisely the imagination-side substrate that ARC-070 needs to operate on. Strong source quality (Nature, single-unit electrophysiology with clean methodology). Mapping fidelity moderate-strong: the paper does not directly demonstrate decomposition-on-prediction-failure but it does demonstrate the substrate (forward sweeps) on which ARC-070's decomposition would operate.
failure_signatures: If REE's hippocampal rollout substrate produces only static / single-shot trajectories without the kind of dynamic elaboration Pfeiffer & Foster document, ARC-070 cannot operate on the substrate as designed -- decomposition presupposes a forward-sweep mechanism that produces variable-grain trajectories. The substrate (ARC-007 hippocampal proposals) needs to support the dynamic-elaboration property documented here.

If the forward-sweep mechanism is constant-grain (single-action timesteps with fixed temporal compression), ARC-070's variable-grain decomposition will not have a substrate to operate on. The hippocampal rollout substrate needs to support multi-grain trajectory representation, consistent with Pfeiffer-Foster's evidence of cues elaborating sequence content.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Before goal-directed navigation in an open arena, the rat hippocampus generates brief sequences of place-cell activity encoding spatial trajectories. These sequences are biased to progress from the rat's current location toward a known goal location and predict immediate future behaviour, including for novel start-goal combinations. The sequences are not pre-stored replays but constructed forward sweeps that integrate the current position, goal location, and intervening map. The sequences extend through the environment with cue-dependent elaboration, suggesting a flexible trajectory-finding mechanism rather than rigid playback.
mapping.ree_translation: Pfeiffer & Foster 2013 documents the rodent analog of REE's hippocampal rollout substrate. The paper supplies two architectural anchors for ARC-070. First, R3 (hierarchy depth) gets indirect support: the forward-sweep substrate is dynamic and elaborates as the trajectory encounters prediction-relevant cues, which is the rodent-electrophysiology counterpart to the multi-level decomposition Badre & D'Esposito document in frontal cortex. The hippocampus is contributing to the same multi-grain control machinery. Second, R4 (mid-execution decomposition) gets a partial anchor: the forward sweeps are generated during navigation, including during ongoing motor activity, so the substrate supports both pre-commitment and (potentially) mid-execution re-elaboration. The mapping to ARC-070 specifically: when a chunked primitive in REE's rollout fails to ground (V_s drops), the hippocampal forward-sweep mechanism that generated the chunk in the first place is the natural locus for re-segmenting it into finer primitives, because that is the same machinery that produced the original chunked sequence.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Pfeiffer & Foster show forward sweeps for goal-directed navigation; they do NOT show a decomposition operation explicitly triggered by prediction failure. The paper supplies the substrate evidence (forward sweeps elaborate trajectories) but not direct evidence of the trigger mechanism (V_s drop -> decompose). The R4 mid-execution-decomposition mapping is inferential: the paper shows sweeps generated during navigation, which suggests the substrate is available for mid-execution use, but it does not demonstrate decomposition firing mid-execution. Additionally, the rodent navigation substrate is one specific instantiation of the hippocampal rollout machinery; whether the same dynamic-elaboration property generalises to other domains (motor sequences, abstract task hierarchies) is an extrapolation supported by the broader episodic-simulation literature but not by this paper alone.
mapping.source_context: Empirical paper, Nature 2013. Single-unit hippocampal recordings in rats during open-field goal-directed navigation. Methodologically clean: clear evidence of forward sweeps that predict immediate future behaviour, including in novel configurations.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high (Nature paper, clean single-unit recordings, replicated and extended by follow-on work). Mapping fidelity moderate-strong: documents the substrate (forward sweeps) on which ARC-070 operates, but not the decomposition trigger directly. Transfer risk moderate-high: rodent open-field navigation is one specific instantiation; whether the dynamic-elaboration property generalises to abstract task hierarchies is an open extrapolation.
